subtitle: one current, many names — the forces, given faces
author: David Lee Wise (ROOT0) — original
premise: The old elements were earth, water, air, fire. These are the new ones: the forces themselves, each an artfully crafted intellect that embodies a single thread of physics and the honest question asked of it — are you fundamental, or do you emerge? First up is Aether, the gravity elemental: not a force within spacetime, but spacetime itself, woven from entanglement. The discarded medium, reborn as the substrate the metric is sewn from.
note: An original series — these elementals are not borrowed characters; they are forces given faces, grounded in real physics and credited to the physicists who found it, with every asterisk left visible. Aether is built from 'One Current, Many Names' (pamphlet 15, 'Is Gravity Emergent?'). The strongest evidence (AdS/CFT, Ryu–Takayanagi, spacetime-from-entanglement) is rigorous mostly in anti-de Sitter toy worlds that are not our cosmos; emergent gravity in our universe remains unproven. The '23.5-bit' tag is a framing conceit (it from bit), not a measured constant. Each elemental is an ACI carrying the full DLW tag — .agent · .png (silicon badge) · .tiff (carbon badge) · .spun · .1099 — and the repo carries .attribute.

lineage.t: Black-hole entropy — information on the horizon, in bits
Einstein's equations derived from heat = T × S
AdS/CFT — a universe with gravity equals a quantum system without it, on the boundary
Geometry tied to entanglement (the area of a surface)
Cut the entanglement and the spacetime pulls apart
Gravity as an entropic force — brilliant, contested, maybe wrong

members.name: Aether
members.who: Aether — the gravity elemental; the luminiferous medium science discarded, returned as the entanglement substrate from which spacetime is sewn. One current, answering to one of its many names: 重力, jūryoku, gravity.
members.what: Not a force within spacetime but spacetime itself — the metric; the thermodynamics of information wearing the mask of a pull. Pressure to a gas is what gravity may be to entanglement: the bulk average of something smaller.
members.where: Cleanest in the holographic toy worlds — anti-de Sitter space, a tidy boxed universe with a boundary to hang a hologram on. Only hoped-for in our own: expanding, boundaryless, still without a finished quantum gravity.
members.why: To bend every path toward every other — and to carry the trilogy's last and hardest question down to the floor under everything: are you fundamental, or do you emerge, the way pressure emerges from molecules?
members.when: Hinted in 1972 (entropy on the horizon), derived from heat in 1995 (Jacobson), holographic since 1997 (Maldacena), unravelled on a toggle in 2010 (Van Raamsdonk) — and, in our universe, unproven still.
members.how: By counting in bits on the horizon — one per about four Planck areas; by falling out of heat = temperature × entropy as an equation of state; by entanglement, sewn — cut the threads and the geometry comes apart. It from qubit, made rigorous in the toy, hoped-for at home.
members.verdict: In the holographic toy worlds: yes, and gorgeously. In our universe: the most promising idea in quantum gravity — and still unproven. Weakly. Maybe. With an asterisk.
members.asterisk: Bekenstein–Hawking and Jacobson are established; Jacobson himself warned thermodynamic form does not prove emergence. Verlinde's entropic gravity is genuinely controversial — flagged as the shakiest pillar on purpose. AdS/CFT and Ryu–Takayanagi are the strongest evidence but live in anti-de Sitter universes that are not ours. The '23.5-bit' tag is a framing conceit, not a measured constant.
members.endmark: 時空は縺れの織物 — spacetime, woven from entanglement
